executive_summary: Latest updates from 2 sources covering the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Monitoring 2 key developments. AI analysis refreshing shortly.
execSummaryRich: Escalation: HIGH. Key developments: ‘Get them out of casinos’: Bannon wants Netanyahu’s son and Gulf royals on Iran . Iran war: How can US military forcibly reopen Strait of Hormuz. Situation remains active.

key_developments.headline: ‘Get them out of casinos’: Bannon wants Netanyahu’s son and Gulf royals on Iran
key_developments.detail: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s son and Arab Gulf royals should be kicked out of the West and put on the front lines of an Iran invasion before the US sends its own troops, Steve Bannon, US President Donald Trump’s former chief strategist, said. key_developments.headline: Iran war: How can US military forcibly reopen Strait of Hormuz
key_developments.detail: As US President Donald Trump rakes up pressure on Iran to sign a deal and end the conflict in West Asia which started when US-Israel launched joint operation against Tehran on February 28, Pentagon is lining up a wide range of military options which could potentially put US troops on ground. terminology_explained.simple_explanation: Iran's elite Revolutionary Guard — controls missiles and drones.
Narrow waterway — 20% of world oil passes through it.

what_to_watch_next: Monitor live feeds for breaking developments.

india_impact.headline: India Monitors Gulf Conflict
india_impact.detail: India's energy security tied to Gulf stability.
india_impact.full_detail: India imports 85% of crude oil from Gulf. Strait of Hormuz disruption would immediately impact supply.

indiaSummary: India continues to monitor the US-Israel-Iran conflict closely given its significant dependence on Gulf energy imports and the large Indian diaspora. Oil price volatility directly impacts petrol prices for Indian consumers.
